marketState: neutral-to-bullish near-term; medium-term bearish headwinds from storage surplus and seasonal weakness
summary: Front NG=F is trading at $3.286, above the fair-value midpoint of $2.960 but comfortably within the model band ($2.46–$3.46). Demand for the next 1–16 days is running well above the 5-year historical maximum (+28.6% and +41.3% respectively), the strongest near-term bullish catalyst in the dataset. Offsetting this, storage is 151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise, and seasonality points firmly lower over the 4-week horizon: only 19% probability of being higher with a typical −1.8% move and LOW reliability. Managed money sits at the 18th 5-year percentile of net positioning (heavily short), creating meaningful short-covering risk on any weather-driven spike but not a structural bull signal given the storage surplus. The short-term setup favours buying dips while extreme heat demand persists; the medium-term balance favours gradual price erosion once the weather premium fades.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal headwinds dominate the 3-12 week horizon: only 19% probability of being higher in 4 weeks, a typical −1.8% four-week move, and storage running 151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise. Price is already above fair-value midpoints by $0.13–0.34 across all forward windows and the extreme heat premium is unlikely to persist into September. The crowded MM short at the 18th 5y percentile limits immediate downside but does not create a structural bull case at current storage levels; waiting for a better-defined entry avoids fighting seasonal and fundamental headwinds.
longTerm.invalidation: Weekly close above $3.60 sustained by persistent above-maximum heat demand through September, or a significant storage deficit surprise that reverses the 151 Bcf surplus.
longTerm.trigger: Initiate SHORT on a rally to $3.38–$3.45 (approaching the upper fair-value band edge of $3.46) with stop at $3.58, targeting TP1 $3.00 and TP2 $2.70 (approx 1:2.6 and 1:4.5 R:R respectively). Alternatively, enter SHORT on a confirmed daily close below $3.15 as the weather premium fades and seasonal selling accelerates.

shortTerm.stop: $3.10 (below key near-term support; negates weather bull thesis)
shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1–7D and 8–16D demand windows are above the 5-year historical maximum (+28.6% and +41.3%), the most bullish near-term reading in the dataset and a powerful price support while heat persists. The 4h timeframe shows a bullish trend with positive MACD; 5m and 15m RSIs at 37–38 signal short-term oversold conditions that favour a mean-reversion bounce toward the upper fair-value band. Managed money is heavily net short (18th 5y percentile), so a weather-driven move higher risks triggering forced short-covering that could accelerate the advance.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $3.10, or a significant weather-model update dropping 1–7D demand below the 5-year average.

keyDrivers: Near-term power-burn demand +28.6% (1–7D) and +41.3% (8–16D) vs 5y norm — both windows above the 5-year historical maximum
Managed money net short at 18th 5y percentile — crowded positioning creates short-squeeze risk on any bullish weather catalyst
Storage 151 Bcf above 5y average with bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise, a structural headwind for medium-term prices
Seasonality weak at 4-week horizon: 19% probability higher, typical −1.8% move, LOW reliability (51 pp 10–90 spread)
Price within fair-value band ($2.46–$3.46 now) and above midpoint; fair value rises toward $3.15 over 21 days, gradually reducing the overvaluation
risks: Rapid weather normalization — demand already pulled back −11.2 BCF/d in 24h and could accelerate lower, breaking the short-term bull case quickly
Storage surplus could widen further if summer injections consistently beat forecasts, pressuring prices into the September shoulder season
Seasonal reliability is very low (51 pp 10–90 spread) — wide uncertainty in both directions means the seasonal mean-reversion signal could fail or amplify unpredictably
